title: Syngonanthus ottohuberi
taxonomy: Syngonanthus ottohuberi belongs to the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This classification places it within the monocot group of flowering plants. Syngonanthus ottohuberi is further classified as a member of the genus Syngonanthus, a group of flowering plants that are typically found in the Americas. The species is likely to be classified within the tribe Syringarieae or the subfamily Mapanioideae. Further research may be required to determine the specific classification within these groups.
